title: A Screenwriting King and the Queen of Movie Podcasts
format: Two interviews: screenwriter-director David Koepp on his Blumhouse horror You Should Have Left and 30 years of blockbusters, then You Must Remember This host Karina Longworth on Polly Platt.

segments.heading: David Koepp
segments.note: on You Should Have Left and a career in Hollywood

films.title: You Should Have Left
films.note: The new Blumhouse horror, written and directed by Koepp from Daniel Kehlmann's novella, with Kevin Bacon. A marriage-horror pitch that started with Bacon; Koepp cites Rosemary's Baby as his all-time favorite for the domestic-dread setting.

films.title: Jurassic Park
films.note: One of the blockbusters that made his name as a screenwriter.

films.title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park
films.note: The sequel he found much harder than the first; says Spielberg would agree you leave your good ideas on the field the first time.

films.title: Mission: Impossible
films.note: Written alongside Robert Towne in different hotels; he and De Palma stayed 'war buddies.'

films.title: Spider-Man
films.note: The very first one.

films.title: Death Becomes Her
films.note: The Zemeckis collaboration Sean had just rewatched.

films.title: Carlito's Way
films.note: One of the wild De Palma adventures.

films.title: Stir of Echoes
films.note: His Richard Matheson adaptation; a formative influence he speaks about often.

films.title: Secret Window
films.note: His Stephen King adaptation; one of three novels he's adapted.

films.title: The Trigger Effect
films.note: An apocalyptic story cranked down to one small group's point of view; he ties it to the Twilight Zone episode 'The Monsters Are Due on Maple Street' and thinks its best shot is the crane-high highway of cars all fleeing one way.

films.title: War of the Worlds
films.note: Same technique as Trigger Effect: a huge world event seen only through one small group's eyes.

films.title: Premium Rush
films.note: He calls it underrated; the New York bike shoot had nine straight days of ER visits and left Joseph Gordon-Levitt a scar.

films.title: Ghost Town
films.note: The film he's proudest of as a director; written with John Kamps, with a touching Ricky Gervais.

films.title: The Vast of Night
films.note: His last-great-thing pick: a patient, restrained 1950s sci-fi on Amazon, crafty on a tiny budget.

segments.heading: Karina Longworth
segments.note: on You Must Remember This and the season on Polly Platt

films.title: The Last Picture Show
films.note: Polly Platt's production design for Bogdanovich, the work Longworth's season sets out to properly credit.

films.title: Targets
films.note: Platt was a huge part of making it; the season argues her producer-like role went uncredited.

films.title: Directed by John Ford
films.note: Another Bogdanovich film where Platt's contribution wasn't credited.

films.title: What's Up, Doc?
films.note: Platt kept working with Bogdanovich after they broke up personally.

films.title: Paper Moon
films.note: The last of the post-breakup collaborations before the creative split.

films.title: Pretty Baby
films.note: Platt wrote and produced it; Longworth reads it as a critique of Hollywood fetishizing young women, not the pedophilia fest its reputation assumes. Episode six of the season.

films.title: Broadcast News
films.note: Platt, credited only as producer, grabbed a can of red paint and touched up the frame herself before James L. Brooks could call action; currently Longworth's favorite of Platt's films.

films.title: Terms of Endearment
films.note: Platt earned an Oscar nomination ten years after she and Bogdanovich stopped working together.

films.title: Say Anything...
films.note: Among the incredible films Platt worked on even as she never got over losing Peter as a collaborator.

films.title: Bottle Rocket
films.note: Late-career Platt, cited in the episode intro alongside her Bogdanovich and Brooks work.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Longworth's favorite film of all time; the Judy Garland version, which reveals the sickness of stardom while still working as first-rate propaganda for Hollywood.

films.title: The Player
films.note: Another movie-about-movies she loves; Sean agrees.

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: Her recent viewing for an upcoming Spike Lee project.

films.title: Until the End of the World
films.note: Her last-great-thing pick: Wim Wenders' five-hour cut on Criterion, which she now thinks about every day.

TV: The Monsters Are Due on Maple Street

Until the End of the World.title: Until the End of the World
